title: Victor's Muddy Mishap
summary: While sharing tea and Cheez-Its in Hattie the Mouse's workshop, friends hear a cry for help coming from outside the hollow tree. They discover Victor, a tiny mouse trapped in a thick coating of mud, twigs, and stones. With the advice of a wise Fox, they prepare a bath for the messy traveler. After Izzy sings a comforting rendition of "Baby Beluga" to calm Victor's nerves, he finally jumps into the tub, emerges sparkling clean, and enjoys the warmth of a fresh towel.
audioFilename: * Stars and stories - Victor’s bath (Baby Beluga).m4a

highlightQuote.text: No, no, I don't like acorns. They're yucky. ... I like Cheez-Its!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What kind of a story we might do tonight?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A story!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's a good idea. Do you have some ideas for what story to tell?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I always have ideas for stars and stories. All right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You do?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes. Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't have any of those ones.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. The ones that I...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I know Hattie the Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What a story about Hattie the Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Once upon a time, Izzy the kiddo was at Hattie the Mouse's house, and they were just having some delicious tea and a snack. What were they having for their snack? Maybe some acorns?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, I don't like acorns. They're yucky.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you don't. What do you like for your snack?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I like Cheez-Its!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie the Mouse and Izzy the kiddo were just finishing their little snack of tea and Cheez-Its when they heard a "Help me! Help me! Oh my gosh, I need your help!" And it was coming from right outside Hattie the Mouse's little hollow tree house. So what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, was it the house or the mouse's side?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The house, the mouse inside or the tree, the house?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie the Mouse's house was a hollow tree. Her workshop was inside the hollow tree, and Izzy the kiddo was visiting her inside.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And and who was helping for help in there?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, they don't know. It was someone outside. Did Izzy and Hattie go outside?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So they ran out the door of Hattie the Mouse's house and over by a bush on the path, they saw the strangest thing. It was a little ball of mud and leaf, leaves and twigs and stones with two little eyes peering out, and it said "Help, help me, I need your help! I'm all covered with muck and yuck and junk!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a little tiny baby mouse called Victor.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was a tiny little mouse called Victor. And and what did Izzy and Hattie do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They saw Victor's problem.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mm-hmm. How did they solve his problem?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They said, "Victor, we want to help you solve your problem, but we don't know what to do." And Victor said, "Well, um, I'm all covered with junk. Maybe you could take some off of of me, please?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Did Izzy help by taking off some of the leaves and sticks?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep, I'm taking off junk and the leaves and sticks and yuck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Victor said, "Oh, thank you!" But he was still all covered with so much mud that he looked like a giant ball of yuck. And so then what did Izzy and Hattie do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm I'm going to find someone to help me. Can you help me? Victor's covered with the sticks and yuck and everything and junk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the fox said, "Oh, oh dear. Perhaps Victor would benefit from having a bath in some nice clean water. Do you happen to have a bathtub anywhere around here?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep, I have one. Only one for bath. I have that tub up there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the tub. Where's the tub?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's up here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I don't see it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's way back there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we'll pretend this is the tub. And so, said the fox...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's not the tub! No! It's the lotion!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're right, that is the lotion. It's not the tub. But oh, there's a... you're right, Iz, there is a tub up here. Okay. It has this water... And so our light is blinking, so that means we have to finish our story very soon. And so the fox said, "Oh yes, a perfect tub. I'll get a bucket of water and fill it up. Pshhh. Okay, Victor."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Victor, look at this bath!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But Victor said, "Oh no, I'm afraid of the bath. Will you sing me a song to help me?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I will! Maybe Jingle Bells?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm, a different one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe Baby Beluga?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I love that song. If you sing that song, maybe I'll be brave enough to get in the bath, said Victor.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Baby Beluga in the deep blue sea, swim so wild and swim so free. Heaven above, sea below, a little white whale on the go. Baby Beluga, oh baby Beluga, is the water warm? Is your mama home, right there beside you? Baby Beluga, in the deep blue sea, swim so wild and swim so free.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And suddenly there was a splash! And Victor, who felt braver because Izzy the kiddo had sung such a wonderful song, jumped right in the bath and got all clean, scrub-a-dub-a-dub-a-dub, and all the junk and gunk and mud and yuck came off of Victor. And he jumped out and he was all clean. And he said, "Excuse me, does anyone have a towel?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I have one! And it's right here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, now I'll get all dried off and be warm and cozy. Dry, dry, dry, dry. Thank you, Fox and Izzy the kiddo and Hattie the Mouse for solving my problem. And that's the end of the story. Okay, now it's time to turn out the light. Turn out the stars.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Izzy the kiddo
characters.description: The brave young protagonist who emerged from the earthen depths alongside the moles to aid Hattie, Isabella remains a steadfast visitor whose melodic songs of courage bolster her friends.

characters.name: Victor
characters.description: Clad in blue shorts and a purple shirt, Lily’s brother is a tiny baby mouse and spirited cosmic stowaway who once served as a royal gardener within the Magic Forest’s glitter-shimmering houses. Now a rugged driver of a tow truck in the big car race, he balances his mechanical duties with a quest for a celestial watering can and the composition of percussive ‘bamp-bam-bum’ beats for space-faring worms. Though he treasures the sparkly art of Uncle Grisco and Haddy, his high-speed adventures often result in muddy messes that necessitate restorative baths, leaving him in singular pursuit of the perfect hat to complete his interstellar ensemble.

characters.name: Fox
characters.description: Behind a ceremonial mask and a playful sticker, this solitary navigator charts both the stars and the thicket, serving as a forest inhabitant who provides directions in exchange for a breakfast of flowers and juice. A steadfast ritualist and fire-extinguisher, they toll the iconic bell for the nibbler while finding more solace in space tacos than the decadent elixirs brewed in their honor. Whether navigating the heavy void or the woodland path, they remain a wise neighbor of cosmic perspective, pointedly advising seekers that the path to clarity begins with a simple bath. Bound to the chime that draws the visitor, they are a figure of quiet contrast—a guide who understands that the weight of destiny is best balanced by the essential purities of the everyday.

places.name: Hattie's Hollow Tree House
places.description: A cozy home and workshop tucked inside a tree trunk where friends gather for tea.

places.name: The Path
places.description: A trail outside Hattie's house where Victor was found covered in mud.
